name: Brute Force
intuition_and_algorithm:
Initialize an array answer with all elements set to -1 to store the next greater elements.
Traverse the array using a for loop to select the current element.
Use a nested for loop to traverse the right side of the array (from the current element) to find the next greater element.
If a next greater element is found, store it in the answer array and break from the inner loop.
Once the outer for loop ends, return the answer array containing the results.

complexities.time: O(N 2 )  (where N is the size of the given array). Using two nested for loops to find the next greater elements.
complexities.space: O(N) , The space required to store the answer is O(N).

code.python:
class Solution:

    # Function to find the next greater 
    # element for each element in the array
    def nextLargerElement(self, arr):
        
        n = len(arr) # size of array
        
        # To store the next greater elements
        ans = [-1] * n
        
        for i in range(n):
            
            # Get the current element
            currEle = arr[i]
            
            # Nested loop to get the 
            # next greater element
            for j in range(i + 1, n):
                
                # If the next greater element is found
                if arr[j] > currEle:
                    
                    # Store the next greater element
                    ans[i] = arr[j]
                    
                    # Break from the loop
                    break
        
        # Return the answer
        return ans

if __name__ == "__main__":
    n = 4
    arr = [1, 3, 2, 4]
    
    # Creating an instance of 
    # Solution class
    sol = Solution()
    
    # Function call to find the next greater element
    # for each element in the array
    ans = sol.nextLargerElement(arr)
    
    print("The next greater elements are: ")
    for i in range(n):
        print(ans[i], end=" ")

name: Optimal Approach
intuition_and_algorithm:
Initialize an answer array to store the next greater elements for the given array.
Declare a stack data structure to help with the traversal.
Start traversing the array from the back.
For the current element, pop elements from the stack until the top is less than or equal to the current element.
If a greater element is found, store it in the answer array; otherwise, store -1.
Push the current element into the stack while maintaining a decreasing order of elements.
Once the traversal is complete, the answer array will contain the results.

complexities.time: O(N)  (where N is the size of the array). Traversing on the hypothetical array takes O(2N) time and traversing the stack will take overall O(2N) time as all the elements are pushed in the stack once.
complexities.space: O(N)  since the answer array takes O(N) space and the space used by the stack will be O(N) in the worst case.

code.python:
class Solution:

    # Function to find the next greater 
    # element for each element in the array
    def nextLargerElement(self, arr):
        
        n = len(arr) # size of array
        
        # To store the next greater elements
        ans = [-1] * n
        
        for i in range(n):
            
            # Get the current element
            currEle = arr[i]
            
            # Nested loop to get the 
            # next greater element
            for j in range(i + 1, n):
                
                # If the next greater element is found
                if arr[j] > currEle:
                    
                    # Store the next greater element
                    ans[i] = arr[j]
                    
                    # Break from the loop
                    break
        
        # Return the answer
        return ans

if __name__ == "__main__":
    n = 4
    arr = [1, 3, 2, 4]
    
    # Creating an instance of 
    # Solution class
    sol = Solution()
    
    # Function call to find the next greater element
    # for each element in the array
    ans = sol.nextLargerElement(arr)
    
    print("The next greater elements are: ")
    for i in range(n):
        print(ans[i], end=" ")
